zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Beeston於1395年出生在英國英格蘭爾貝斯頓時，她的父親John Beeston25歲，母親Margaret Hulgreve24歲。她於1409年與Robert De Aston III在英國爾盧可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1455年10月17日享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Beeston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of the many places in England so called. Those in Nottinghamshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire were settlements where bent grass grew (Old English bēos ‘rough grass’ + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’). In Bedfordshire it probably denoted a settlement on a bend (Old English byge + genitive singular -es + tūn). The hamlet stands on a marked bend of the river Yvel. In Cheshire it is named from a rock, below which trading took place (Old English byge ‘commerce’ + Old English stān ‘stone’). The reference is to a prominent crag where the remains of Beeston Castle stand. Compare Beaston and Beeson .
occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived in that part of a village known in Middle English as bi este toun ‘(place) at the east (end) of the village’.

variants.name: Beston
meanings: English: variant of   Beeston   and   Beaston  .

variants.name: Beeson
meanings: English: variant of   Beeston  , a habitational name from a place called Beeston. The spelling reflects the local pronunciation of the Nottinghamshire placename, although this form is now quite widespread in England.

variants.name: Beaston
meanings: English: variant of   Beeston   (the more common form of the surname in England), a habitational name from any of various places called Beeston.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Beeston was born in 1395, in Beeston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Beeston, was 25 and her mother, Margaret Hulgreve, was 24. She married Robert De Aston III in 1409, in Runcorn, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 17 October 1455, at the age of 60.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Beeston(이)가 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 브이스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Beeston(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Hulgreve(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1409년 영국 체실르 런코른에서 Robert De Aston III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1455년 10월 17일 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Robert De Aston III em 1409, em Runcorn, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de outubro de 1455, com 60 anos.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert De Aston III в 1409 г. в местности рункорн, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 17 октября 1455 г. в возрасте 60 лет.
